raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/XXXX, my Citibank credit card was withdrawn {$1000.00} without my consent. I contacted the bank immediately and disputed the transaction, and got a new card. I did everything I could do. The transaction still appears after XXXX weeks. The case is still pending. I contacted the bank regarding this. The representative told me I need to wait for the result, and it is about 60-90 days. My problem is why I need to wait for the result, while the bank can just ask me for money first? Can all people just pay {$1000.00} ahead if they just live paycheck to paycheck? In addition, I went to the police station for this matter too. The policer said they need a filled-out form from the bank and to bring it in with me. However, I asked a Citi online representative, and He or she said " we do not issue a particular form ''. The same thing happened to me around XXXX, Covid pandemic period with a XXXX checking account. I went to the police station to report and was asked to fill out a form with the bank first. Why do steps become so complicated when people want to fix the fraudulent activity, but the criminal did those bad things easily?
